summary: V2 D5 prose still says sidecars 'before lock'; fix to match D4.5 (after-lock). Internal spec contradiction missed in V2 edit.

V2 D5 frontmatter example stores linkable_name=Welcome (capital W) but key is welcome (lowercase) — inconsistent. Pin: linkable_name VALUE is canonical case (e.g., 'welcome'); index KEY is lowercased version of same.

importlib.util loads wiki-write.py as 'wiki_write' module; running CLI is '__main__'. Exception classes from both are distinct id()s. Fix: extract slugify + exception classes + build_frontmatter into scripts/_wiki_common.py (NEW shared module); both wiki-write.py and _wiki_migrate.py import from it.

ArchiveThenRenameOp execution has two steps (archive existing target, then rename source) but journal only records phase:rename. Crash between archive and rename can't be reconstructed. Fix: extend journal schema with phase:archive row written before the phase:rename row; resume handles both.
Resume schema-validation must accept empty journal + valid sidecars as no-op exit 0 (NOT MigrationJournalCorruptError). Crash between sidecar-write and first journal append leaves this state.

Pre-migration vault index must include OLD basenames as synthetic aliases so refs like [[PatternCall — iOS Native Rewrite]] resolve to the migrated page DURING Phase B (alias-injection writes the canonical-slug alias to the page, but Phase B runs the resolver BEFORE the injection step in some orderings).

Same-second --migrate invocations collide on <UTC-ts>/<rel-path> archive paths. Add uniqueness via pid suffix OR fail if archive target exists. Recommend: fail-if-exists keeps the implementation simple and is the safer default for data-protection code.

Markdown range scanner: pin exact rules for backtick vs tilde fences, fence length matching, EOF-unclosed fences, CRLF handling, frontmatter-only-at-byte-0, inline code spans, callout fences (> ```). Add as design subsection with edge-case fixture table in T5.

MigrationPlan.__post_init__ 'no path appears in two buckets' rule is ambiguous with ArchiveThenRenameOp (archive_from and rename_op.new_path are the same path BY DESIGN). Pin: validation skips paths inside ArchiveThenRenameOp.archive_from when checking renames bucket.
AC #11 sub-case (y) for exit-1 sub-causes needs to assert stderr substring per CG-1's 6 messages. Make it 6 explicit sub-cases (y1-y6) or one parameterized case with 6 inputs.
T3 (run_lint tail line) has no accompanying test case. Add to test-wiki-write.sh: assert lint output ends with the suggested --migrate command line when violations > 0.

Split T5 (450-550 LoC monolith) into T5a (constants + dataclasses + import setup, ~100 LoC) and T5b (logic — compute_plan, build_vault_index, resolvers, scanner). T5a runs first; T5b + T6 + T7 can run in parallel with shared dataclasses.
Document in design.md the carve-to-slice point if T5b stalls during /build (per slice-strategy-for-autorun-build memory).

T2b + T2c + T12b add --alias support to default-write — new v0.17.0 surface. Optional: defer to v0.17.1 (post-build evaluation; if /build wave 1 lands cleanly, keep; if stalls, this is the natural carve).
